Open Source Intel Fox News: Israel quietly supported the U.S. rescue of a downed airman inside Iran, according to Fox News. Israeli officials shared intelligence with U.S. forces as the operation unfolded and helped limit Iranian air activity in the area during the roughly 36 hour rescue window. tweet

Michael A. Horowitz RT @amaherYAH : the Americans have always excelled in surgical ops from the iraq war to venezuela and iran. but the air power strategy has never succeeded in achieving strategic goals in the mideast. chaos is a repeated potential consequence. Fox News can confirm that the 2nd crew member of the downed F15E fighter jet has been rescued and he and the members of the rescue team that extracted him from behind enemy lines in Iran are all safely out of Iran. That according to two senior US officials and multiple well placed sources in the region. The Weapons Systems Officer ejected along with the pilot when their F15E Strike Eagle they were flying was struck Thursday night (early Friday local time) in southwest Iran. The WSO used the SERE (Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape) training to evade capture, hiding on an elevated ridge after hiking away from the wreckage and putting out an emergency beacon.) US Special Operations rescue forces to include PJs (United States Air Force Pararescuemen (PJs) and many layers of elite rescue forces took part in the complex, layered mission to both find the crew member and also keep the Iranian forces who were hunting the American weapons system operator at bay. There are videos that have appeared from local eyewitnesses that show what appear to have been injured and dead Iranian members of the IRGC and Basij who were looking for the downed American crew member. Fox has learned there was fighting on the ground but no Americans killed during the operation. “It was a very complex operation to retrieve the downed service member,” a well placed source briefed on the operation told me. Many different branches of the US military were involved in the rescue. Fox News can confirm the A10 Warthog that crashed Friday was involved in providing cover for the rescue teams searching for the pilot. That A10 crashed in Kuwait (first reported by ABC Friday) but the A10 pilot managed to eject safely and was rescued. There was destruction of aircraft which have sensitive equipment on board, I am told, all part of this complex CSAR (Combat Search and Rescue) mission. The F15E was pretty much destroyed on impact. Two rescue helicopters were hit by enemy fire on Friday and crew members onboard were injured by enemy fire but managed to make it out of Iran. There were a lot of elements to this rescue, I am told. - Jennifer Griffin tweet

Coordination with UNIFIL continues throughout the fighting in southern Lebanon. tweet

Reviewing U.S. hostile policies from Carter to Biden, he said each administration entered the field thinking it faced a weak Iran, but reality proved otherwise. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 Ukrianian President Zelenskyy stated in a interview with AP that Russia has provided Iran with satellite intelligence on approximately 50‑53 Israeli energy sites, enabling strikes on civilian infrastructure with no military purpose. He stated that all of Russia’s experience gained in the war against Ukraine, including Shahed drones, is being transferred to Iran. Zelenskyy added that Ukraine can share its experience of reopening the Black Sea grain corridor using naval drones, but no country has requested help with the Strait of Hormuz. @wfwitness

🇮🇱 Specialists in chemical protection suits removed a smoking parcel containing toxic materials from Ben Gurion Airport. The baggage area was cordoned off and people were evacuated. It turned out that the smoking parcel contained a material vapors of which are dangerous even when inhaled, according to Ynet. The origin of the dangerous cargo is being investigated. Emergency services of Israel are working at the scene. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

The U.S. government asked satellite companies to restrict imagery of the Iran conflict, and Planet Labs is now limiting access. They company is moving to “managed access”, images released only case-by-case. Delays extended (up to ~2 weeks for new imagery).

Iran launched overnight drone/missile strikes on Kuwait, UAE, and Bahrain, targeting energy infrastructure. Kuwait: Oil Ministry building, power plants, desalination sites, oil facilities hit → outages, fires. UAE: gas/energy sites targeted → fires, some casualties from debris. Bahrain: refinery/storage sites hit → fires, repeated sirens.

U.S. forces conducted a deep incursion ~400 km inside Iran, south of Isfahan, a highly sensitive area with nuclear facilities, missile bases, and an airbase. The goal was to recover a downed F-15E jet pilot, using a temporary desert airstrip set up in a sparsely populated region. The operation relied on surprise and remote terrain to avoid detection. Multiple aircraft and helicopters were allegedly involved. But leaving the area became difficult, suggesting complications during extraction. Implications (if true): Would indicate significant weaknesses in Iran’s air defense coverage, even near strategic sites. Raises questions about response time and detection capability. Hypothetically shows how such an operation could be used for more strategic objectives (e.g., seizing Iran's enriched uranium stockpiles). Coordinates: 32.258394, 51.901927 (andynovy at X)

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 Fars report on the latest search operations for the American pilot The continuation of the search operation for the American pilot in the provinces of Isfahan and Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad was accompanied by the destruction of several invading drones and helicopters; according to the spokesperson of the Central Headquarters of Khatam al-Anbiya, the enemy's attempt to rescue the downed fighter pilot failed, and Trump is trying to justify the bitter defeat and failure of his cowardly army by creating ambiguity for public opinion. Since Saturday night, search operations to find the American pilot or pilots have continued in the three provinces of Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari, Isfahan, and Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad. — Isfahan: Around 2 a.m. today, residents of southern Isfahan reported hearing the sound of an airplane flying, and simultaneously, the sound of drones and helicopters was also heard in parts of Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad. According to local sources, until about 5 a.m., several explosions were heard in southern Isfahan; in this regard, the IRGC and the army announced in statements the destruction of 2 MQ-9 drones and Hermes 900, and the Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters also reported the destruction of 2 transport aircraft and 2 helicopters. Some local sources reported the enemy's attempt to destroy the remains of the crashed aircraft and even the bodies of the killed American forces. Field sources also report the killing of several American forces in this successful operation by the country's armed forces. — Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad: During the enemy's early morning attack on a point in Siyah Mountain, a point in Kakan, and the Vozg area of Yasuj city, a total of 9 martyrs and 7 wounded. Some media linked these attacks to the search operation for the pilot and even claimed that American forces were deployed to rescue the American pilot, but official provincial sources denied these claims. The political and security deputy governor of Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad told a Fars reporter: No heliborne operations were carried out by the enemy during last night's attacks. — Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari: Investigations by the Fars reporter regarding some media fabrications about Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province and clashes there indicate that these claims are false. Hours ago, Trump, in a media move and without releasing any image of the pilot, claimed to have rescued the second pilot.
